title: sports-registry — realtime broadcasts
description: The sports-registry module does NOT broadcast on realtime channels directly. Sport / Discipline / Position mutations fire domain events (see `events.json`) which downstream modules (notifications, activity, teams) may republish over their own broadcast channels — but this module ships zero channels of its own. Broadcast surfaces belong to the domain that owns the read side (team-picker UI, catalog admin UI), not the write side.
notes: If the SPA needs live updates when a platform admin adds a new Sport to the catalog, it subscribes to the standard settings-changed channel + the tenant-scoped cache-invalidation broadcast the settings module already ships.
The Team module (Wave 2b) will ship a broadcast channel for sport-picker updates when its cache invalidates — but that channel lives with Team, not here.

SportPrefixResolutionFailed events are audit-critical + fire SportPrefixResolutionFailedNotification via the notifications module — that notification is the tenant-admin-facing realtime signal, not a raw broadcast.
